message: fix(btrfs): fail closed on extent allocation scan errors (bd-mr2nv)

files.patch:
@@ -948,6 +948,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-mlyx","title":"Reject invalid RAID5/6 stripe counts in btrfs mapping","description":"Enforce minimum stripe counts for RAID5/6 in logical stripe mapping and cover with tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T12:53:19.458973173Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T13:03:07.027049236Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T13:03:07.026797060Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-mmplg","title":"add CLI and runtime log-contract coverage for ext4 MMP refusal states","description":"Survey finding: ext4 MMP read-side validation is implemented and there is already an open conformance bead (`bd-h4vn9`) for active-MMP write-open rejection, but the operator-facing surface still lacks explicit CLI/runtime log-contract coverage for fsck/active/unsafe MMP refusal paths.

Scope:
- Add focused tests that open or mount ext4 images with `MMP` in `Clean`, `Fsck`, `Active`, and `UnsafeUnknown` states.
- Verify user-visible error messages and errno mappings remain stable for write-participating flows.
- Assert structured tracing fields for refusal paths, including status class and sequence when present.
- Keep clean-state behavior unchanged.

Acceptance:
- The operator-facing mount/open path has deterministic coverage for each non-clean MMP state.
- Structured logs surface enough detail for incident/debug use without raw block dumps.
- The new coverage complements, rather than duplicates, the lower-level conformance bead.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T05:05:36.397146438Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T05:05:36.394608536Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","logging","mount","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mmplg","depends_on_id":"bd-h4vn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:31:17.412740808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-mq8t","title":"Reject multi-device chunks in map_logical_to_physical","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T00:00:16.399867333Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T00:00:18.855426699Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T00:00:18.855164071Z","close_reason":"Validate RAID profile/stripe counts for map_logical_to_physical and add tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-mr2nv","title":"Fail closed on btrfs extent-tree allocation scan errors","description":"Btrfs extent allocation currently treats extent_tree.range errors as an empty allocation set, which can allocate over occupied space if the in-memory tree is structurally invalid. Propagate the scan error and add a regression that failed allocation leaves accounting and delayed refs unchanged.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-fs","created_at":"2026-04-27T07:07:26.973901646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T07:12:04.693543781Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T07:12:04.691444174Z","close_reason":"Propagated btrfs extent-tree allocation scan errors instead of treating them as an empty allocation set, with a regression proving corrupted allocator tree state fails closed without accounting or delayed-ref side effects. Validation passed: focused ffs-btrfs regression, fmt, diff-check, UBS touched file, workspace check, and workspace clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ms69","title":"Complete btrfs multi-device RAID + send/receive + final workspace cleanup","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:53:51.235948109Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T08:54:03.635219786Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T08:54:03.634732833Z","close_reason":"Final parity session:
1. BtrfsDeviceSet: multi-device read abstraction with stripe fallback for mirrored RAID profiles
2. parse_send_stream(): btrfs send stream parser (22 command types, TLV attribute encoding, 3 tests)
3. All clippy lints cleaned across modified crates
4. FEATURE_PARITY: 86/86 tracked features
Test results: 120 ffs-btrfs + 361 ffs-ondisk + 180 ffs-journal + 474 ffs-core = 1135 pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-msaku","title":"add FS_IOC_GETFSLABEL ioctl support in ffs-fuse","description":"Survey finding: FS_IOC_GETFSLABEL (0x81009431) is a common read-only ioctl that returns the filesystem label. Neither ffs-fuse nor ffs-core currently implement this.

Scope:
- Add FsOps::get_fs_label() trait method with default EOPNOTSUPP impl
- Implement ext4 path returning s_volume_name from superblock (up to 16 bytes, null-terminated)
- Implement btrfs path returning label from superblock (up to 256 bytes)
- Wire FS_IOC_GETFSLABEL dispatch in ffs-fuse ioctl handler
- Add unit dispatch tests in ffs-fuse
- Add mounted-path E2E test in fuse_e2e.rs using fslabel/getfattr

Acceptance:
- FS_IOC_GETFSLABEL returns correct label for ext4 and btrfs images
- Direct dispatch tests verify encoding and buffer handling
- Mounted-path test confirms real kernel/FUSE round-trip","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T11:23:00.697131691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T11:39:50.655132026Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T11:39:50.652616215Z","close_reason":"Implemented FS_IOC_GETFSLABEL ioctl for ext4 and btrfs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-msta","title":"Evidence tail=0 should skip ledger scan","description":"Avoid reading entire evidence ledger when --tail=0; return empty after validating file exists.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-11T14:06:53.701444454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T14:15:46.865164370Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T14:15:46.864992305Z","close_reason":"Skip ledger scan for tail=0 and harden evidence tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
